title: iberis spathulata subsp. lereschiana
intro: Iberis spathulata subsp. lereschiana, also known as Rock Cress, is a flowering plant species in the Brassicaceae family. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin issues and wounds due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Rock Cress is valued for its potential health benefits, which include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and providing antioxidant effects. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, infusions, and topical creams, allowing for various uses in traditional medicine.
